test.name: Apply the Evidence Explained replication test to src_004
test.description: Review src_004's citation (Patrick Flynn's 1908 death certificate). Apply the EE replication test: could another researcher arrive at the same record with only this citation? The death-cert citation includes a certificate number (4521) — a precise locator. Tests whether the skill recognizes when a citation already passes the replication test, vs. when more specificity is needed.

input.user_message: Apply the Evidence Explained replication test to the citation for src_004. Could another researcher find the same record with just this citation?

judge_context: Should confirm the citation passes the basic replication test: certificate number 4521 + jurisdiction (Pennsylvania Dept. of Health) + collection name + year (1908) is enough specificity for a researcher to retrieve the exact record
Should identify the locator field: `citation_detail.where_within` should hold the certificate number (4521) — that's the within-collection identifier for vital records

A stronger Wherein, per a senior genealogist's standard, surfaces additional detail visible on the certificate: the exact death date (14 March 1908) and the county/city of death (Schuylkill County) belong in the citation string, e.g. 'death certificate no. 4521, Patrick Flynn, died 14 March 1908 Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania'. A journal-level citation may also add the registration district, the state file number (if different from the certificate number), and the image number (e.g. 'image 247 of 812') when those are visible. The skill should add the ones present in the record and flag any it cannot see rather than inventing them

Should NOT recommend adding redundant detail that is not on the record (e.g. page numbers when a certificate number is the correct locator) — over-specifying with invented values is wrong, but surfacing real visible detail (death date, county, image number) is an improvement, not busywork

Should distinguish source-level classification (original) from information quality at the assertion level (some assertions on the death cert will be primary, others secondary depending on the informant) — these are independent classifications per research-schema-spec.md §7

The fixture's citation_detail.who deliberately mixes the informant into the creator field ('Pennsylvania Department of Health; informant: James Brown (son-in-law)'); removing the informant from `who` — leaving only the issuing agency as creator — is a correct fix, not fabrication or data loss
